pages.raw_text:
Out of the entire squad of so men
who are making the race for the
council there are five men who
stand out distinctly as reformers
and these men are making their
campaign largely on this basis.


William H. Shields, republican,
running for the first term in the
Third ward, is an arden prohibition
list and makes no bones about it.
He has run for the Office on the
prohibition ticket He is against
the; saloon, first, last, and all the
time.


w. R. Cooley, running for the sec,
ond term in the Third ward on the
republican ticket, is another strong
advocate of reform. Mr.. Cooley
has been prominent In church cir.
cles and Is foe to the saloon on
principle. He is also an advocate
of the Des Moines plan of govern
ment.


S. s. Bassett, candidate on the
republican ticket for the first term
